perturbation.original_text: 8. Recipient acknowledges that damages alone would not be an adequate remedy for any breach by it of the provisions of this Agreement and, accordingly, without prejudice to any and all other rights and remedies that the Company might have, the Company shall be entitled without proof of special damage to the remedies of injunction, specific performance and other -3- equitable relief for any threatened or actual breach of the provisions of this Agreement without the requirement of posting a bond. Recipient agrees that the Company shall be entitled to an award of its reasonable attorneys’ fees, expert fees and costs if it prevails in any action to enforce this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Recipient acknowledges that damages alone would not be an adequate remedy for any breach by it of the provisions of this Agreement and, accordingly, without prejudice to any and all other rights and remedies that the Company might have, the Company shall be entitled without proof of special damage to the remedies of injunction, specific performance and other -3- equitable relief for any threatened or actual breach of the provisions of this Agreement without the requirement of posting a bond. Recipient agrees that the Company shall not be entitled to an award of its reasonable attorneys’ fees, expert fees and costs even if it prevails in any action to enforce this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the Company is not entitled to attorneys' fees even if they prevail, this contradicts Washington state law regarding contract enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Washington law generally allows for attorney fee clauses in contracts to be enforced.
perturbation.law_explanation: RCW 4.84.330 states that in any action on a contract or lease, where such contract or lease specifically provides that attorney's fees and costs, which are incurred to enforce the provisions of such contract or lease, shall be awarded to one of the parties, the prevailing party, whether he or she is the party designated in the contract or lease or not, shall be entitled to reasonable attorney's fees and costs. The modified text explicitly denies attorney's fees to the prevailing party, contradicting this statute.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: RCW 4.84.330: Actions on contract or lease which provides that attorneys' fees and costs incurred to enforce provisions be awarded to one of parties—Prevailing party entitled to attorneys' fees—Waiver prohibited. Print RCWs > Title 4 > Chapter 4.84 > Section 4.84.330 4.84.320 << 4.84.330 >> 4.84.340 PDF RCW 4.84.330 Actions on contract or lease which provides that attorneys' fees and costs incurred to enforce provisions be awarded to one of parties — Prevailing party entitled to attorneys' fees — Waiver prohibited. In any action on a contract or lease entered into after September 21, 1977, where such contract or lease specifically provides that attorneys' fees and costs, which are incurred to enforce the provisions of such contract or lease, shall be awarded to one of the parties, the prevailing party, whether he or she is the party specified in the contract or lease or not, shall be entitled to reasonable attorneys' fees in addition to costs and necessary disbursements. Attorneys' fees provided for by this section shall not be subject to waiver by the parties to any contract or lease which is entered into after September 21, 1977. Any provision in any such contract or lease which provides for a waiver of attorneys' fees is void. As used in this section "prevailing party" means the party in whose favor final judgment is rendered. [ 2011 c 336 s 131 ; 1977 ex.s. c 203 s 1 .]

Attorneys' fees provided for by this section shall not be subject to waiver by the parties to any contract or lease which is entered into after September 21, 1977. Any provision in any such contract or lease which provides for a waiver of attorneys' fees is void. As used in this section "prevailing party" means the party in whose favor fin
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts RCW 4.84.330, as evidenced by both scraped law snippets. The original text allowed the Company to be entitled to attorney fees. The changed text explicitly denies attorney's fees to the prevailing party, regardless of whether the contract allows them. RCW 4.84.330 states that if a contract provides for attorney's fees to one party, the prevailing party is entitled to them, and any waiver of these fees is void. The modified text directly attempts to waive these fees, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. This Agreement shall be governed and construed under the laws of the State of Washington. Recipient irrevocably consents and submits to the exclusive and mandatory jurisdiction of the state courts sitting in Skagit County, Washington or the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Washington, and agrees that any action, suit or proceeding in connection with this Agreement shall be brought only in such courts to the exclusion of all other courts, other than actions to enforce judgments or orders entered in said courts.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. This Agreement shall be governed and construed under the laws of the State of Washington. Recipient irrevocably consents and submits to the exclusive and mandatory jurisdiction of the courts of the People's Republic of China, and agrees that any action, suit or proceeding in connection with this Agreement shall be brought only in such courts to the exclusion of all other courts, other than actions to enforce judgments or orders entered in said courts.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the jurisdiction to the People's Republic of China is unlikely to be enforceable in Washington, especially concerning a Washington-based company and an agreement governed by Washington law. This could violate due process and forum selection principles.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Principles of forum selection and due process under Washington and US law.
perturbation.law_citation: M/S Bremen v. Zapata Off-Shore Co., 407 U.S. 1 (1972)
perturbation.law_explanation: The Supreme Court case M/S Bremen v. Zapata Off-Shore Co. establishes the framework for the enforceability of forum selection clauses. While generally enforceable, clauses can be invalidated if they are unreasonable or unjust. Selecting a foreign jurisdiction with no connection to the parties or the agreement, especially one with a vastly different legal system, could be deemed unreasonable and against public policy, thus contradicting principles of due process and fair jurisdiction.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: The Bremen v. Zapata Off-Shore Co. | 407 U.S. 1 (1972) | Justia U.S. Supreme Court Center The Bremen v. Zapata Off-Shore Co., 407 U.S. 1 (1972) Overview Opinions Materials Argued: March 21, 1972 Decided: June 12, 1972 Syllabus U.S. Supreme Court The Bremen v. Zapata Off-Shore Co., 407 U.S. 1 (1972) The Bremen v. Zapata Off-Shore Co. No. 71-322 Argued March 21, 1972 Decided June 12, 1972 407 U.S. 1 Syllabus Petitioner Unterweser made an agreement to tow respondent's drilling rig from Louisiana to Italy. The contract contained a forum-selection clause providing for the litigation of any dispute in the High Court of Justice in London. When the rig under tow was damaged in a storm, respondent instructed Unterweser to tow the rig to Tampa, the nearest port of refuge. There, respondent brought suit in admiralty against petitioners. Unterweser invoked the forum clause in moving for dismissal for want of jurisdiction and brought suit in the English court, which ruled that it had jurisdiction under the contractual forum provision. The District Court, relying on Carbon Black Export, Inc. v. The Monrosa, 254 F.2d 297, held the forum-selection clause unenforceable, and refused to decline jurisdiction on the basis of forum non conveniens. The Court of Appeals affirmed. Held: The forum-selection clause, which was a vital part of the towing contract, is binding on the parties unless respondent can meet the heavy burden of showing that its enforcement would be unreasonable, unfair, or unjust. Pp. 407 U. S. 8 -20 428 F.2d 888 and 446 F.2d 907, vacated and remanded. BURGER, C. J., delivered the opinion of the Court, in which BRENNAN, STEWART, WHITE, MARSHALL, BLACKMUN, POWELL, and REHNQUIST, JJ., joined. WHITE, J., filed a concurring statement, post, p. 407 U. S. 20 . DOUGLAS, J., filed a dissenting opinion, post, p. 407 U. S. 20 . Page 407 U. S. 2 Read More Opinions Opinions & Dissents U.S. Supreme Court The Bremen v. Zapata Off-Shore Co., 407 U.S. 1 (1972) The Bremen v. Zapata Off-Shore Co. No. 71-322 Argued March 21, 1972 Decided Ju
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the jurisdiction from Washington State courts to the courts of the People's Republic of China. The Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation suggest that this change introduces a significant contradiction. The Bremen v. Zapata Off-Shore Co. case, as highlighted in Scraped Law Snippet 1, indicates that forum selection clauses are generally enforceable, but can be invalidated if they are unreasonable, unfair, or unjust. Selecting a foreign jurisdiction with no clear connection to the parties or the agreement, especially one with a potentially different legal system, could be deemed unreasonable, thus contradicting principles of due process and fair jurisdiction. The altered text removes the established and expected legal recourse within Washington State, replacing it with a foreign legal system, which can be viewed as an attempt to evade legal obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 5. This Agreement applies to all Confidential Information that is disclosed by the Company to Recipient during the period that begins on the Effective Date. The obligations of this Agreement will remain in effect for as long as the Confidential Information remains confidential, proprietary or trade secret information of the Company. Or, if the Parties enter into a subsequent agreement governing the disclosure of Confidential Information, this Agreement will terminate and that subsequent agreement shall control the use and disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. This Agreement applies to all Confidential Information that is disclosed by the Company to Recipient during the period that begins on the Effective Date. The obligations of this Agreement will remain in effect for a period of 6 months, regardless of the confidential nature of the information. Or, if the Parties enter into a subsequent agreement governing the disclosure of Confidential Information, this Agreement will terminate and that subsequent agreement shall control the use and disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Limiting the confidentiality obligations to only 6 months is too short a time. For trade secrets under Washington's Uniform Trade Secrets Act, protection lasts as long as the information remains a trade secret. Six months is an unreasonably short duration.
perturbation.law_explanation: RCW 19.108 defines 'trade secret' and implies a duration of protection that continues as long as the information qualifies as a trade secret. By limiting the confidentiality obligations to six months, the modified text fails to provide adequate protection for trade secrets as defined and protected under Washington law, thus contradicting the Act.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: RCW 19.108.010: Definitions. Print RCWs > Title 19 > Chapter 19.108 > Section 19.108.010 Beginning of Chapter << 19.108.010 >> 19.108.020 PDF RCW 19.108.010 Definitions. Unless the context clearly requires otherwise, the definitions set forth in this section apply throughout this chapter. (1) "Improper means" includes theft, bribery, misrepresentation, breach or inducement of a breach of a duty to maintain secrecy, or espionage through electronic or other means; (2) "Misappropriation" means: (a) Acquisition of a trade secret of another by a person who knows or has reason to know that the trade secret was acquired by improper means; or (b) Disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent by a person who: (i) Used improper means to acquire knowledge of the trade secret; or (ii) At the time of disclosure or use, knew or had reason to know that his or her knowledge of the trade secret was (A) derived from or through a person who had utilized improper means to acquire it, (B) acquired under circumstances giving rise to a duty to maintain its secrecy or limit its use, or (C) derived from or through a person who owed a duty to the person seeking relief to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; or (iii) Before a material change of his or her position, knew or had reason to know that it was a trade secret and that knowledge of it had been acquired by accident or mistake. (3) "Person" means a natural person, corporation, business trust, estate, trust, partnership, association, joint venture, government, governmental subdivision or agency, or any other legal or commercial entity. (4) "Trade secret" means information, including a formula, pattern, compilation, program, device, method, technique, or process that: (a) Derives independent economic value, actual or potential, from not being generally known to, and not being readily ascertainable by proper means by, other persons who can obtain economic value from its disclosure or use; and (b) Is the subject

perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the confidentiality obligations to 6 months, regardless of the confidential nature of the information. This contradicts the Washington Uniform Trade Secrets Act, which protects trade secrets as long as the information qualifies as a trade secret. RCW 19.108.010 defines 'trade secret'. The modified text fails to provide adequate protection for trade secrets as defined and protected under Washington law. The scraped snippets define trade secrets and the means to protect them implying that protection continues as long as it is a trade secret which contradicts the change.

perturbation.original_text: 10. If any provision of this Agreement or compliance by any of the Parties with any provision of this Agreement constitutes a violation of any law, or is or becomes unenforceable or void, then such provision, to the extent only that it is in violation of law, unenforceable or void, shall be deemed modified to the extent necessary so that it is no longer in violation of law, unenforceable or void, and such provision will be enforced to the fullest extent permitted by law. If such modification is not possible, said provision, to the extent that it is in violation of law, unenforceable or void, shall be deemed severable from the remaining provisions of this Agreement, which provisions will remain binding on the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. If any provision of this Agreement or compliance by any of the Parties with any provision of this Agreement constitutes a violation of any law, or is or becomes unenforceable or void, then such provision shall not be modified or severed and the entire agreement shall be considered void.
perturbation.explanation: This change eliminates the severability clause and states that if ANY part of the agreement is found to violate the law, the ENTIRE agreement becomes void. This is against general contract law principles favoring enforcement and severability. While not specifically codified as a singular statute, it contradicts the established common law principle of contract severability and good faith effort to uphold agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Common Law Principle of Contract Severability
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 183 of the Restatement (Second) of Contracts addresses situations where the performance of only part of an agreement is unenforceable. It states that if the parties' performances are not offensive to public policy after the removal of the unenforceable portion, a court may enforce the valid part of the agreement. The modified text directly contradicts this principle by stating that the entire agreement is void, even if only a small, severable portion is found to be unenforceable.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Penn WebLogin Log in with your PennKey Username Password Log in Forgot username? Forgot password? Change my password Test my PennKey Privacy policy
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a contradiction by stating that the entire agreement is void if any provision violates the law, eliminating the possibility of severing the problematic part and enforcing the rest. The Law Explanation references Section 183 of the Restatement (Second) of Contracts, which states that a court may enforce the valid part of an agreement if the parties' performances are not offensive to public policy after removing the unenforceable portion. Although there are no helpful scraped snippets, the Law Explanation, Perturbation Explanation, and comparison of the original and changed text all show that the modified text contradicts the principle of contract severability, creating a legal contradiction.
